title: integration: Skip TestProxyMetadata on non-amd64 architectures
body:
Skip TestProxyMetadata on non-amd64 architectures.

Otherwise test fails on other architectures like explained here: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2829

comments.body:
Verification runs:
- aarch64: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5768477
- ppc64le: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5768476

@ricardobranco777 you need a rebase.  Thoughts on @mtrmac 's last comment.
LGTM once that's settled.

> @ricardobranco777 you need a rebase. Thoughts on @mtrmac 's last comment. LGTM once that's settled.

Just rebased.  I tried the suggested approach in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2834 but it fails when I backport it to v1.20.0.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
@ricardobranco777 thanks for the background.  The tests are very unhappy; they look like flakes.  I've restarted.

@ricardobranco777 Why shouldn’t we use use #2834? Even if that doesn’t work for a backport, I think you would be backporting something that does not exist in an upstream release _either way_, and either way after you update, the backport can be removed. I don’t see that the backport situation makes much of a difference to anyone (other than I’m somewhat curious why it is failing there). Am I missing something?

> @ricardobranco777 Why shouldn’t we use use #2834? Even if that doesn’t work for a backport, I think you would be backporting something that does not exist in an upstream release _either way_, and either way after you update, the backport can be removed. I don’t see that the backport situation makes much of a difference to anyone (other than I’m somewhat curious why it is failing there). Am I missing something?

I'm not saying we shouldn't use it, just that I have no way to verify it works on non-amd64 hosts, short of compiling skopeo.  Otherwise I think we can merge this to skip the test until I can verify the other works.
